message.content:
Implementation files: src/claim-office.ts, src/cli.ts
Test files: src/claim-office.spec.ts
Passing tests: 38

Run the final metric-driven refactoring pass over the whole src/.
Iterate ONE change at a time with pre/post measurement (ESLint smells, SonarJS
cognitive complexity, APP mass, McCabe cyclomatic complexity). Stop when no
metric improves further or no improvement is possible.

Notes:
- Run tests with `pnpm test` (must stay 38 passing/green after every change).
- The project uses ESLint 9 flat config (eslint.config.mjs) with typescript-eslint and eslint-plugin-sonarjs. Use `npx eslint src/` to measure smells/cognitive complexity.
- src/claim-office.ts holds all the domain logic (premium quoting + claim processing). src/cli.ts is a thin stdin/stdout wrapper.
- Do NOT change behavior or the public API (runScenario signature, CLI I/O format). Keep all tests green.

EXPERIMENT MODE: Run autonomously, return when done.

attachment.content:
- green: TDD Green Phase
- red: TDD Red Phase
- test-list: TDD Test List Phase
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text:
## Iteration 1 — Pre-Measurement

### Step 0: ESLint Smells (whole src/)

There is exactly **1 ESLint smell** in the entire production tree:

| File | Line | Rule | Message |
|------|------|------|---------|
| src/claim-office.ts | 70:33 | `no-magic-numbers` | No magic number: 6 |

That is `Number(amount.toFixed(6))` in `roundAfterStrippingNoise`. No cognitive-complexity violations anywhere (all functions well below the threshold of 10). cli.ts is clean.

### Step 1: Naming Evaluation

Walking every function across both files with the full 38-test suite in view:

- `runScenario`, `handleQuote`, `handleClaim` — accurate dispatch/handler names.
- `quotePremium`, `itemsPremium`, `policyBasePremium`, `componentBasePremium` — precise.
- `assertKnownItemTypes`, `assertClaimValid`, `assertDamageAmountsNonNegative`, `assertDamagesWithinInsuredCounts` — clear assertion intent.
- `roundPremiumUp` / `roundPayoutDown` / `roundAfterStrippingNoise` — reveal both the direction and the floating-point-noise concern.
- `reimbursementRate`, `damagePayout`, `incidentPayout`, `insuranceSum` — domain-accurate.
- cli.ts: `readStdin`, `main` — standard, clear.

No rename improves intent. The per-cycle refactors already named everything well. **Decision: keep all names.**

### Step 2 & 3: APP Mass + McCabe per function

Computing for the functions with any branching/notable mass:

| File | Function | APP Mass (approx) | McCabe |
|------|----------|------|--------|
| claim-office.ts | `roundAfterStrippingNoise` | ~8 | 1 |
| claim-office.ts | `componentBasePremium` | ~12 | 2 |
| claim-office.ts | `countBy` | ~24 | 2 (loop + `??`) |
| claim-office.ts | `assertKnownItemTypes` | ~13 | 2 |
| claim-office.ts | `assertDamagesWithinInsuredCounts` | ~22 | 2 |
| claim-office.ts | `itemSurchargeRate` | ~20 | 3 (two `?:` via rateWhen args + `===`/`>=`) |
| claim-office.ts | `reimbursementRate` | ~7 | 2 |
| claim-office.ts | `quotePremium` | ~28 | 1 |
| claim-office.ts | `handleQuote` | ~30 | 1 |
| claim-office.ts | `handleClaim` | ~30 | 1 |
| cli.ts | `readStdin` | ~14 | 2 (loop) |
| cli.ts | `main` | ~12 | 1 |

Max McCabe is **3** (`itemSurchargeRate`, structurally trivial — a flat sum of three additive rates, inherently clear). Max cognitive complexity is well under the threshold of 10. No function exceeds `max-lines-per-function` (30), `max-depth` (3), or `max-params` (4).

The **only** measurable, actionable signal is the single `no-magic-numbers` smell on line 70. Every other metric is already at or near its floor. Let me address that one smell.

### Step 4: Apply ONE refactoring — extract the `6` literal to a named constant

The `6` is the number of decimal places retained when stripping floating-point noise. Extracting it to a named constant reveals intent (Rule 2) and removes the smell.

content.content:
TOTAL SMELLS: 0
---
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  38 passed (38)

content.text:
**Iteration 1 — Refactoring Applied**: Extracted magic literal `6` to named constant `NOISE_FREE_DECIMALS` in `roundAfterStrippingNoise` (src/claim-office.ts)

**Pre/Post Deltas**:
- ESLint smells (whole src/): **1 → 0** (removed `no-magic-numbers`)
- Cognitive complexity (`roundAfterStrippingNoise`): 0 → 0 (unchanged)
- APP mass (`roundAfterStrippingNoise`): ~8 → ~8 (literal `6` became a binding, mass-neutral)
- McCabe (`roundAfterStrippingNoise`): 1 → 1 (no branches changed)
- Net new smells introduced anywhere: **none**

**Tests**: 38/38 passing ✅

Continue to next iteration.

## Iteration 2 — Pre-Measurement

### Step 0: ESLint Smells

**Total smells across whole src/: 0.** No cognitive-complexity violations, no max-lines, max-depth, max-params, duplication, or magic-number violations anywhere.

### Step 1: Naming Evaluation

Re-walked all functions (verified in Iteration 1). All names reveal intent given the full test suite. No rename warranted.

### Step 2 & 3: APP + McCabe

Re-checked the table. Max McCabe = 3 (`itemSurchargeRate`), max cognitive complexity well under threshold (10), max APP mass ~30 (`handleQuote`/`handleClaim`/`quotePremium`) — all minimal for the behaviour expressed.

Looking for further minimization angles:
- **Guard clauses / nested-if flattening**: no nested conditionals exist (max-depth never approached). Nothing to flatten.
- **switch → lookup table**: there are no switch statements; the one `if`-chain candidate (`reimbursementRate`) is a single guard returning a rate, already minimal and deliberately structured for priority-ordered clauses (per its comment, future clauses slot in).
- **Cross-file duplication (Rule 3)**: cli.ts and claim-office.ts share no logic — cli.ts is a pure I/O wrapper, claim-office.ts is pure domain. No extractable cross-file duplication exists.
- **Fewest elements (Rule 4)**: every helper is referenced; no dead abstractions. Collapsing small helpers (e.g. `rateWhen`, `applySurcharge`) into call sites would *reduce* clarity and *increase* duplication — a Rule 2/Rule 3 regression for no metric gain.

Any further change would trade clarity or duplication against an already-floored metric set, with no net benefit.

### Iteration 2 — No Further Improvement Possible

- ESLint smells (whole src/): **0**
- Max cognitive complexity across all functions: well below the SonarJS threshold of 10
- Max APP mass: ~30 (`handleQuote`/`handleClaim`/`quotePremium`) — minimal for the behaviour expressed
- Max McCabe cyclomatic: **3** (`itemSurchargeRate`) — structurally a flat additive sum
- All function names accurately describe their purpose given the full 38-test suite

**Reasoning**: Every metric is at or near its floor. The single actionable smell was removed in Iteration 1. No nested conditionals to flatten, no switch chains to table-ize, no cross-file duplication to extract, no dead abstractions to remove. Any further change would reduce clarity or introduce duplication with no measurable benefit.

End-refactor pass complete.

---

## 🏁 End-Refactor Complete

**Iterations applied: 1**

**Per-iteration summary:**
1. `roundAfterStrippingNoise` (src/claim-office.ts) — extracted magic `6` → `NOISE_FREE_DECIMALS`. Smells [1→0], cognitive [0→0], APP [~8→~8], McCabe [1→1].

**Whole-src final state:**
- Total ESLint smells: **0**
- Max cognitive complexity: under threshold (no `sonarjs/cognitive-complexity` violations)
- Max APP mass: ~30 (in `handleQuote` / `handleClaim` / `quotePremium`)
- Max McCabe cyclomatic: **3** (in `itemSurchargeRate`)

**Tests: 38/38 passing ✅**

The production tree was already in strong shape thanks to the per-cycle refactors (well-named domain helpers, no cross-file duplication between the thin `cli.ts` wrapper and the `claim-office.ts` domain core, no nested conditionals or switch chains). The whole-src measurement pass surfaced exactly one residual smell — a magic number that a per-cycle refactor had left — which was eliminated. All other metrics were already at their floor.

Relevant files:
- /home/experimenter/experiments/runs/2026-05-30_04-34-58_claim-office-example-mapping_v6.5-end-refactor_opus-4-8-no-thinking/src/claim-office.ts
- /home/experimenter/experiments/runs/2026-05-30_04-34-58_claim-office-example-mapping_v6.5-end-refactor_opus-4-8-no-thinking/src/cli.ts
